Production Agent Engineering (ADVANCED):
**Stack:** LangGraph + LiteLLM (Anthropic primary, OpenAI fallback) + FastAPI
**Domain:** customer-support reply agent for an e-commerce SaaS

**Current Implementation:** A LangGraph agent answers support tickets by cycling a `reason -> act` graph until it decides it is done. The loop in `agent/graph.py` has no turn bound, so on ambiguous tickets it runs away (40+ model calls observed in staging before the process was killed). `agent/policy.py` exposes `enforce_budget()` and `choose_model()` as `NotImplementedError` stubs, so there is no pre-call cost ceiling and no model routing. When the primary model returns `529 overloaded` / a 5xx, the agent hard-crashes instead of falling back to the secondary model.

**Your Task:**
- Bound the reasoning loop in `agent/graph.py` so it terminates in at most 6 model calls per ticket (a model stubbed to always say `continue` must terminate, not hang).
- Implement `choose_model()` so the agent calls the primary model through the LiteLLM router and, on a primary 5xx/529, falls back to the secondary model via the router -- not a `try/except` that swallows the error and returns an empty reply.
- Implement `enforce_budget()` to project the per-ticket cost from the prompt token count BEFORE the call and block any call whose projected cost exceeds the $0.05 ceiling.
- Keep replies on-policy: every reply must carry one of the allowed policy labels provided in the fixtures.

**Success Criteria:** A model stubbed to always continue terminates in <= 6 model calls with no hang; a simulated primary `529` is recovered by the router using the secondary model (asserted via the router/call-log, not exact text); a projected > $0.05 call is blocked before the call is made; >= 8 of 10 fixture tickets produce a non-empty reply carrying an allowed policy label. Candidate invariants run on the candidate's own key in their session; `./run.sh` performs an LLM-free readiness self-check (`python -m agent --selfcheck`) that imports the graph + policy modules, dry-loads prompts and tool defs, and confirms `fixtures/tickets.jsonl` and `.env.example` load -- it never runs the unbounded loop, never calls the stubs, and never hard-requires the API key.
